question: Compare the NuScale vs PWR parameters from Table 1.3-1 with the NuScale RPV dimensions from Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in core size, output, safety systems, and key components, showing that NuScale uses a smaller, simpler, and more integrated design with fewer external systems compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for clarity.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures for maintaining the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel. It details the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation over its 60-year design life, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document for regulatory purposes, and all copies must retain the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, which covers the summary description and design basis of the system.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features. It indicates whether each guide is applicable, not applicable, or partially/conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further details. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially met or fully conformed to, demonstrating NuScale’s approach to meeting regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component, supporting various internal systems and ensuring the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, with materials and fabrication standards adhering to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0135.title: Table 1.9-3: Conformance with NUREG-0800, Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS)
nuscale_ch01_0135.summary: Table 1.9-3 summarizes NuScale’s conformance with the regulatory criteria outlined in NUREG-0800 (SRP) and DSRS, indicating whether each section’s acceptance criteria are met or applicable. The table shows that NuScale generally conforms to relevant SRP sections, while criteria specific to Combined License (COL) applicants or those not referencing the Design Certification Application (DCA) are marked as not applicable.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and chapters for further information on radioactive waste management and building layouts.

nuscale_ch01_0768.title: DSRS 15.5.1-15.5.2, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System Malfunction That Increases Reactor Coolant InventoryII.4.C Input Parameters and Initial Conditions - Core Burnup
nuscale_ch01_0768.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding chemical and volume control system malfunctions that increase reactor coolant inventory, specifically focusing on input parameters and initial conditions such as core burnup. It states that NuScale’s design conforms to the relevant DSRS sections, and clarifies that certain SRP guidance related to pressurizer relief valves is not applicable due to differences in NuScale’s safety valve design. Overall, the section documents compliance and notes any exceptions or inapplicability.

nuscale_ch01_0829.title: SRP 17.5, Rev 1: Quality Assurance Program Description - Design Certification, Early Site Permit and New COL applicantsII.D Procurement Document Control
nuscale_ch01_0829.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 17.5, Rev 1 requirements for quality assurance in procurement document control and related areas. Most criteria are met, but some responsibilities—such as site-specific document control, identification and control of materials, special processes, and certain inspection provisions—are assigned to the COL (Combined License) applicant referencing the certified design. The table clarifies which requirements are fully, partially, or not applicable to NuScale’s design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures that list and explain the diagrams and symbols used, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards relevant to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases related to materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures for pressurized water reactors.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: Threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components use Alloy 718, chosen for its compatibility with borated water and resistance to stress corrosion cracking, and comply with ASME BPVC standards. Preservice and inservice inspections and testing of pressure-retaining components follow ASME BPVC Section XI and relevant regulatory requirements, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program to ensure ongoing integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

edges.description: Section A provides nominal design parameters for the NuScale and typical PWRs (including RPV dimensions), which are directly compared to the detailed NuScale RPV dimensions found in Section B, allowing verification that the summarized values in Table 1.3-1 align with the specific design data in Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1.

Section A references Table 1.3-1 to provide nominal NuScale and PWR parameters for comparison, while Section B introduces Chapter Five, which contains the detailed NuScale RPV dimensions in Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1, thus the parameters in Section A can be directly compared to the specific RPV dimensions detailed in Section B.

Section A provides comparative design parameters for NuScale and PWRs, which can be used to assess whether the NuScale RPV dimensions (from Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1) meet the regulatory radiation protection design features referenced in Section B.

Section A presents nominal design parameters for NuScale and PWRs in Table 1.3-1, which can be directly compared to the specific NuScale RPV dimensions detailed in Section B's Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1, connecting general comparative data with specific design details.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications for reactor vessel integrity, which are detailed in the broader context of the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application introduced in Section B.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity by not exceeding pressure-temperature limits, while Section B lists regulatory guidance and the degree of conformance related to radiation protection design features, with both sections referencing compliance with regulatory requirements but focusing on different aspects (mechanical integrity vs. radiation protection).

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which directly depends on the reactor vessel's design and configuration as described in Section B.

Section A provides the structural context and copyright information for the NuScale reactor coolant system documentation, while Section B gives a detailed description of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), with both sections being parts of the same regulatory document and Section A serving as the preface to the technical details found in Section B.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and conformance statements relevant to radiation protection design features, while Section B describes the physical configuration and components of the NuScale reactor vessel, with the relationship being that the design parameters and dimensions in Section B must satisfy the regulatory requirements and guidance referenced in Section A.

Section A documents the NuScale FSAR's conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B provides a comparison of NuScale and PWR design parameters, which serve as evidence supporting the conformance claims in Section A.

Section A documents NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications, thus Section B provides evidence that supports the conformance claims made in Section A.

Section A documents NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria (such as NUREG-0800), while Section B is the preface to the technical chapter containing the detailed design information (including RPV dimensions) that supports and substantiates the conformance claims in Section A.

Section B's Table 1.3-1 provides nominal NuScale and PWR parameters, which can be compared to the NuScale RPV dimensions referenced in Section A's general arrangement discussion, connecting overall plant layout to specific design values.

Section A provides the general arrangement and layout context for major structures and equipment, which includes the reactor vessel whose integrity and operational procedures are specifically addressed in Section B, connecting the physical plant layout to the operational safety requirements for the reactor vessel.

Section A provides a general overview and references figures and tables (such as Table 1.3-1) that summarize plant parameters, while Section B introduces Chapter Five, which contains detailed technical information (including Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1) about the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV) dimensions, thus Section A's summary is structurally linked to Section B's specifics.

Section B provides nominal design parameters for NuScale and PWRs in Table 1.3-1, which can be directly compared to the NuScale RPV dimensions found in Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1, thus allowing Section A's analysis of input parameters and initial conditions to be contextualized with actual design values.

Section A provides input parameters and initial conditions (such as core burnup and initial power level) for accident analysis, which are directly influenced by the reactor vessel dimensions and integrity described in Section B, as these physical parameters constrain and inform the selection of appropriate analysis values.

Section A references regulatory criteria and input parameters for safety analysis, while Section B provides the source documentation (including tables and figures) containing the NuScale RPV dimensions needed to compare with the parameters in Table 1.3-1, thus Section B supplies the technical details that Section A requires for analysis.

Section A documents the NuScale FSAR's conformance with regulatory criteria (such as NUREG-0800), while Section B provides a general description and layout of major plant structures, with both sections connected by the need to demonstrate that the plant's physical arrangement (Section B) supports compliance with the regulatory requirements outlined in Section A.

Section A documents the NuScale FSAR's overall conformance with regulatory criteria, including DSRS sections like those in Section B, while Section B details specific conformance with DSRS 15.5.1-15.5.2 input parameter requirements, thus Section A references and summarizes the compliance status that Section B provides in detail.

Section A provides the general arrangement and layout context for the NuScale plant, which is necessary to interpret and apply the input parameters and initial conditions for accident analyses described in Section B.

Section B provides comparative design parameters for NuScale and typical PWRs (including RPV dimensions), which can be cross-referenced with the detailed NuScale RPV dimensions found in Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1, while Section A addresses the quality assurance and procurement document control processes that ensure the accuracy and regulatory compliance of such design data.

Section A outlines the quality assurance requirements for procurement and documentation that must be met during design certification, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity, both of which are connected by the need for controlled documentation and compliance with technical specifications to maintain safety and regulatory conformance.

Section A describes the quality assurance and document control requirements that must be met for the design certification process, while Section B introduces the NuScale plant design documentation that is subject to those quality assurance controls.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify design information, which can be used to interpret and contextualize the NuScale and PWR parameters listed in Section B's comparative table.

Section A provides drawings and figures that visually clarify design information, which can be used to interpret and support the reactor vessel integrity requirements and procedures discussed in Section B.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify design information referenced in Section B's preface to the reactor coolant system chapter, supporting the understanding of parameters and dimensions discussed therein.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical basis for the design parameters and material choices compared in Section B's Table 1.3-1, thereby supporting the validity of the NuScale vs PWR parameter comparison.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical and quality assurance requirements for reactor coolant boundary integrity, which Section B relies on when describing the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity and compliance with technical specifications.
Section A cites external references that provide technical and regulatory background supporting the content and claims presented in Section B's preface and subsequent chapters.

Section B provides nominal design parameters for NuScale and PWRs in Table 1.3-1, which can be compared to the specific RPV fastener material and design details described in Section A, connecting general plant parameters to detailed component specifications.

Section A describes the material and design standards for RPV threaded fasteners, which are critical to maintaining the reactor coolant pressure boundary integrity that Section B seeks to protect through operating procedures ensuring pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded.

Section A discusses the design and material requirements for RPV threaded fasteners and references Section 3.13 for further details, while Section B is the preface to the chapter containing those detailed design descriptions, establishing that Section A's content is elaborated within the scope introduced by Section B.

Section A provides drawings and figures that visually clarify design information referenced in Section B, which lists standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary.

Section A provides general design drawings and figures that visually clarify component positions, while Section B specifies detailed material and code requirements for threaded fasteners used in components depicted in those drawings, connecting the visual representation to specific design criteria.

Section A cites industry standards and guidelines (such as ASME and EPRI) that are referenced in Section B to justify the selection and qualification of threaded fastener materials for the RPV and related components.
